core_corpus_rule: Use the language in which Spinoza composed the text whenever the authorial-language text survives.
llm_wiki_rule: Include auditable authorial-language texts and contemporary or early historical textual witnesses; exclude modern translations from the default research corpus.

roles.author_original: Core corpus and default LLM citation source.
roles.text_witness: Eligible for the LLM wiki when provenance and historical relation to the lost authorial text are documented.
roles.historical_translation: A contemporary or early translation eligible for the LLM wiki, while remaining distinct from authorial-language text.
roles.modern_translation: Reading aid excluded from the default LLM wiki source corpus.
roles.ai_translation_or_explanation: Dynamic interpretive output that must link back to source passages.
roles.scan_or_unverified_ocr: Reference or work material, excluded from the core corpus until verified.

requirements: Record source URL or source file identifier.
Record text_role and llm_wiki_eligible when the relation to the authorial text is not self-evident.
Do not use a translation as the preferred source when an authorial-language text is available.
Mark lost, uncertain, incomplete, translated, or indirect texts explicitly.
Do not exclude a contemporary historical translation merely because the authorial-language original is also available.
